We offer you the top attraction near Saona Island , which is the Natural Pool, a shallow sandbar located in the middle of the Caribbean Sea. This is typically a stop on boat tours to Saona Island. The water here is very shallow (waist-deep in most places) and crystal clear, making it a perfect spot to relax and swim. The natural pool is also known for its starfish, which can be found in the sand. You will also have your typical Dominican Lunch, Rum, soda and water. The return will be on board of a Catamaran where you will be entertained by the staff making sure that you had a very wonderful day

What are people saying about Punta Cana

Half Day Dominican Republic Cultural Tour : Small Group
Mary_R, May 2026
A wonderful experience
Our tour guide Wester is amazing! He goes above and beyond giving us the best experience we could have. He even bought us mangoes to keep when we are asking for one. Horse back riding is a plus to me. He makes sure somebody stays with me and guiding the horse so I would feel comfortable and have the best experience. Market tour is good too. I’ve learned a lot of local spices and fruits. I highly recommend him

La Hacienda Park: 7in1 Zipline, Chairlift, ATV, Ride Horse & More
Ashlee_G, May 2026
10/10! We loved here definitely plan on bringing the rest of our family and friends here. Beautiful staff, Thank youu!!!
A time to remember! My husband and I will definitely be back again for this amazing experience! The staff made sure this excursion was the best in DR for sure! To able to do participate in seven different things for only $100 per person, I see that as a huge steal and we enjoyed every bit of it. From the horses, to the buggies, chairlift, 5 different zip lines, safari/petting zoo, and food⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Thanks to others coming here and posting their experiences at this beautiful place I’m glad I booked and I’m even happier my husband enjoyed this for his birthday❤️

ATV & Buggy adventure in Punta Cana + Water Cave + Macao Beach
Laura_D, May 2026
We had such a fun time on...
We had such a fun time on the ATV & buggy excursion in Punta Cana! From start to finish, everything was well organized, including the hotel pick-up which made it super convenient. The buggy ride itself was definitely the highlight expect to get muddy, but that’s part of the fun! The route takes you through beautiful rural areas, and it really feels like an adventure. The stop at the water cave was refreshing and unique and Macao Beach was absolutely stunning perfect spot to relax and take pictures. The guides were friendly, helpful, and kept everything running smoothly while making sure everyone was safe and having a good time. A quick tip: bring sunglasses, a bandana or face covering, and clothes you don’t mind getting dirty. Overall, such a great experience and totally worth it if you’re visiting Punta Cana and want something fun and different!

Best boat trip in Punta Cana
100% recommend this tour! The staff were friendly and fun. The snorkeling was fantastic, there were hundreds of fish which would eat directly from your hand including angel fish and puffer fish. Looking out onto the ocean as we travelled to each destination, I saw multiple turtles which was really special. The tour was fun and well organized!

Buggy Tour Punta Cana: MagicalCaves ,Dream Beaches & Culture
Orsasha_M, Apr 2026
Awesome tour and company
This was an amazing experience from beginning to the end. The crew were excellent. Driving/riding the buggy was so much fun. Getting covered in the mud made it even more exciting. Make sure to wear old clothes, water shoes, goggles, bandanda. Also bring cash just in case you want to make any purchases since there are a few stops along the route. It was just myself and husband and I in our buggy, so we had a 2 seater, but there were also families in a 4 seater buggy with younger kids. I definitely recommend this tour and company.

Punta Cana: El Dorado Water Park Entry Ticket with Transportation
Monique_b, Apr 2026
El Dorado Water Park
I had an excellent time at El Dorado Water Park in Punta Cana. The park was clean, well-maintained, and offered a great variety of attractions for both relaxation and excitement. The slides were thrilling, the lazy river was perfect for unwinding, and there were plenty of areas to just sit back and enjoy the atmosphere. The staff were friendly and helpful, which made the experience even more enjoyable. It can be a little confusion because most of the workers does not speak English, Overall, it was a fun and memorable experience, and I would definitely recommend El Dorado Water Park to anyone visiting Punta Cana.

Were these results helpful?
en
4cb51726-bafa-460c-9ea4-e9eca269b285
things_to_do

Punta Cana perches prettily on the eastern tip of the Dominican Republic and is the gateway to some of the Caribbean island’s best beaches. Go where cars can’t on these Punta Cana tours, which take you to turquoise swimming holes, secluded beaches, and coffee and cacao plantations. Turn up the volume on a catamaran equipped with an open bar and DJ booth, and heighten your chance of spotting tropical marine life on a snorkel tour.

People Also Ask

What are the best tours in Punta Cana?

There are lots of tours that let you experience the best of Punta Cana. Looking at recent ratings and reviews, here are the five most popular tours right now:

  • Adventure Bundle: Monkeyland, Ziplines & Buggies Punta Cana
  • Full-Day Santo Domingo City Tour from Punta Cana
  • Punta Cana Zipline Adventure with Buggies and Horseback Riding
  • Half-Day Adventure 4x4 ATV, Water Cave and Dominican Culture
  • Monkeyland and Plantation Safari Tour from Punta Cana
Are guided tours in Punta Cana worth it?

Yes, guided tours help you explore more of Punta Cana and include adventures like ATV rides, snorkeling trips, and horseback riding. Laidback options such as coffee and cacao tastings or catamaran cruises offer family-friendly options and make for a great rest day.

Per one customer who booked the Adventure Bundle: Monkeyland, Ziplines & Buggies Punta Cana tour, “Fabulous experience. It poured for the monkeys and ATVs, but all of the guides made it so enjoyable. Honestly all 3 activities were so much fun. Our main man Sammy was so knowledgeable. I highly recommend all 3 activities.”

How much do Punta Cana tours cost?

It depends on the activity. Inexpensive souvenir shopping trips can cost as little as US$10. On the high end, private tours can cost hundreds. Most tours fall somewhere in between, with options that include add-ons like hotel pickup and drop-off, food and drinks, or admission tickets typically costing more.

On the lower end of the price range, you can book tours such as a Shopping Tour in Don Lucas Cigar Factory, Rum Tasting, Cocoa Factory, Souvenirs for around US$5 per person. This tour takes you to a number of shopping and sampling spots, but transportation is not included. Pricier options include VIP tours such as Luxury Private Yacht Rental in Punta Cana - Up to 12 People (from US$14,830 per group), which includes a lavish yacht with a captain to take you to secret coves and beaches for the day.

What activities are included on Punta Cana tours?

Some Punta Cana excursions bundle together exciting outdoor adventures like mud buggy rides, ziplines, and cave tours. Others are focused on water activities like snorkeling, parasailing, and catamaran tours. And if you consider day drinking an activity, you can’t miss Punta Cana tours out on the water with an open bar.

Consider an all-day or multi-day tour to pack in as many sights and activities as possible. For example, the Discovery Package Saona Island & ATV From Punta Cana tour includes pickup, an ATV tour of Saona Island, a barbecue lunch, and a stop at a natural pool. Plus, you can do it all again and visit a different area of the island the next day.

How many days in Punta Cana is enough?

If you’re visiting Punta Cana as part of a longer trip that includes additional destinations, 2–4 days is plenty of time. But if you’re spending your entire trip in Punta Cana, plan to stay for at least five days to explore all that the area has to offer.

What is the best month to visit Punta Cana?

December to April is considered an ideal time to visit Punta Cana, when the dry season is in full swing. Be sure to check the weather before booking a trip from June to November, as this is when hurricanes are most likely.

What are the best attractions in Punta Cana?

According to travelers, these are the best attractions in Punta Cana:

  • Macao Beach (Playa Macao)
  • Bavaro Beach (Playa Bavaro)
  • Coco Bongo Punta Cana Tours
  • Hoyo Azul Lagoon
  • Saona Island (Isla Saona)

Terms & ConditionsHow Viator works
By clicking “Accept All”, you agree to the storing of cookies on your device to enhance site navigation, analyze site usage, and assist in our marketing efforts. Privacy and Cookies Statement

Privacy Preference Center

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change the default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
Privacy and Cookies Statement

Manage Consent Preferences

Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms.    You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

Functional Cookies

These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages.    If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.

Targeting Cookies

These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites.    They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

Performance Cookies

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site.    All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
